<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 4 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月4日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 4일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 4 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初四]][[zh:三月初四]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 4 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月4日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 4일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 4 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初四]][[zh:三月初四]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 4 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月4日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 4일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 4 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初四]][[zh:三月初四]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838975]] Wikidata name: 4th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 4|qq=March 4}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初四|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 4일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 4''' is the 4th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}}.
== Events == * [[:en:天喜|天喜]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1053|1053]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The great Amitābha Hall (鳳凰堂) at Heidōin (平等院) in Uji was completed under the direction of Fujiwara Yorimichi. * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1771|1771]] [[:en:April 18|April 18]]) – Sugita Genpaku and Maeno Ryōtaku observed the dissection of condemned prisoners; the very next day they began translating the work “Tahel Anatomia.” * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:April 9|April 9]]) – Tokugawa Iezawa appointed Matsudaira Sadatoki as his shōgun’s assistant (補佐), marking the beginning of the Kansei reforms. * [[嘉慶 (中国)|嘉慶]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1796|1796]] [[:en:April 11|April 11]]) – In the Qing dynasty, a rebellion was triggered by a group of Bai Lian (White Lotus) adherents—peasant groups forming a Maitreya-based religious association. * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1806|1806]] [[:en:April 22|April 22]]) – A great fire, known as the “Great Cultural Fire,” broke out in Edo. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1837|1837]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – Due to the Great Tenpō Famine, the Tokugawa shogunate established rice relief granaries throughout Edo. * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:April 21|April 21]]) – Tokugawa Iemochi entered Nijō Castle in Kyoto, the first time in 230 years since Shōkō had reigned. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Sunpu Castle, Tetsusaburō Yamaoka met with Saigō Takamori, appealing with a spirit of submission to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers safeguarding the emperor—10,000 strong from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains—were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 10th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1697|1697]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – Kamo Mafuchi, a Kokugaku scholar (<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mabuchi-kinenkan.jp/history/index.html|title=賀茂真淵について|publisher=賀茂真淵記念館|accessdate=2021-05-03|qq=}}</ref>) (died [[:en:1769|1769]]). == Deaths ==
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|3|3|5|2|4|4|3|0304|qq=}}

– The great Amitābha Hall (鳳凰堂) at Heidōin (平等院) in Uji was completed under the direction of Fujiwara Yorimichi. * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1771|en|1771|WD=Q7686|lt=1771}} {{ill|April 18|en|April 18|WD=Q2521|lt=April 18}}) – Sugita Genpaku and Maeno Ryōtaku observed the dissection of condemned prisoners; the very next day they began translating the work “Tahel Anatomia.” * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9}}) – Tokugawa Iezawa appointed Matsudaira Sadatoki as his shōgun’s assistant (補佐), marking the beginning of the Kansei reforms. * [[嘉慶 (中国)|嘉慶]] 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1796|en|1796|WD=Q7818|lt=1796}} {{ill|April 11|en|April 11|WD=Q2501|lt=April 11}}) – In the Qing dynasty, a rebellion was triggered by a group of Bai Lian (White Lotus) adherents—peasant groups forming a Maitreya-based religious association. * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}} {{ill|April 22|en|April 22|WD=Q2526|lt=April 22}}) – A great fire, known as the “Great Cultural Fire,” broke out in Edo. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1837|en|1837|WD=Q7608|lt=1837}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – Due to the Great Tenpō Famine, the Tokugawa shogunate established rice relief granaries throughout Edo. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|WD=Q2525|lt=April 21}}) – Tokugawa Iemochi entered Nijō Castle in Kyoto, the first time in 230 years since Shōkō had reigned. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – At Sunpu Castle, Tetsusaburō Yamaoka met with Saigō Takamori, appealing with a spirit of submission to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – The soldiers safeguarding the emperor—10,000 strong from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains—were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 10th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1697|en|1697|WD=Q7702|lt=1697}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}}) – Kamo Mafuchi, a Kokugaku scholar (<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mabuchi-kinenkan.jp/history/index.html|title=賀茂真淵について|publisher=賀茂真淵記念館|accessdate=2021-05-03|qq=}}</ref>) (died {{ill|1769|en|1769|WD=Q7679|lt=1769}}). == Deaths ==
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|3|3|5|2|4|4|3|0304|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}}.
== Events == * [[:en:天喜|天喜]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – The great Amitābha Hall (鳳凰堂) at Heidōin (平等院) in Uji was completed under the direction of Fujiwara Yorimichi. * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1771|en|1771|WD=Q7686|lt=1771}} {{ill|April 18|en|April 18|WD=Q2521|lt=April 18}}) – Sugita Genpaku and Maeno Ryōtaku observed the dissection of condemned prisoners; the very next day they began translating the work “Tahel Anatomia.” * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9}}) – Tokugawa Iezawa appointed Matsudaira Sadatoki as his shōgun’s assistant (補佐), marking the beginning of the Kansei reforms. * [[嘉慶 (中国)|嘉慶]] 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1796|en|1796|WD=Q7818|lt=1796}} {{ill|April 11|en|April 11|WD=Q2501|lt=April 11}}) – In the Qing dynasty, a rebellion was triggered by a group of Bai Lian (White Lotus) adherents—peasant groups forming a Maitreya-based religious association. * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}} {{ill|April 22|en|April 22|WD=Q2526|lt=April 22}}) – A great fire, known as the “Great Cultural Fire,” broke out in Edo. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1837|en|1837|WD=Q7608|lt=1837}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – Due to the Great Tenpō Famine, the Tokugawa shogunate established rice relief granaries throughout Edo. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|WD=Q2525|lt=April 21}}) – Tokugawa Iemochi entered Nijō Castle in Kyoto, the first time in 230 years since Shōkō had reigned. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – At Sunpu Castle, Tetsusaburō Yamaoka met with Saigō Takamori, appealing with a spirit of submission to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – The soldiers safeguarding the emperor—10,000 strong from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains—were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 10th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1697|en|1697|WD=Q7702|lt=1697}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}}) – Kamo Mafuchi, a Kokugaku scholar (<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mabuchi-kinenkan.jp/history/index.html|title=賀茂真淵について|publisher=賀茂真淵記念館|accessdate=2021-05-03|qq=}}</ref>) (died {{ill|1769|en|1769|WD=Q7679|lt=1769}}). == Deaths ==
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|3|3|5|2|4|4|3|0304|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635368}}.
== Events == * [[:en:天喜|天喜]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26|1=March 26}}) – The great Amitābha Hall (鳳凰堂) at Heidōin (平等院) in Uji was completed under the direction of Fujiwara Yorimichi. * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1771|en|1771|WD=Q7686|lt=1771|1=1771}} {{ill|April 18|en|April 18|WD=Q2521|lt=April 18|1=April 18}}) – Sugita Genpaku and Maeno Ryōtaku observed the dissection of condemned prisoners; the very next day they began translating the work “Tahel Anatomia.” * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788|1=1788}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – Tokugawa Iezawa appointed Matsudaira Sadatoki as his shōgun’s assistant (補佐), marking the beginning of the Kansei reforms. * [[嘉慶 (中国)|嘉慶]] 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1796|en|1796|WD=Q7818|lt=1796|1=1796}} {{ill|April 11|en|April 11|WD=Q2501|lt=April 11|1=April 11}}) – In the Qing dynasty, a rebellion was triggered by a group of Bai Lian (White Lotus) adherents—peasant groups forming a Maitreya-based religious association. * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} {{ill|April 22|en|April 22|WD=Q2526|lt=April 22|1=April 22}}) – A great fire, known as the “Great Cultural Fire,” broke out in Edo. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1837|en|1837|WD=Q7608|lt=1837|1=1837}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – Due to the Great Tenpō Famine, the Tokugawa shogunate established rice relief granaries throughout Edo. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|WD=Q2525|lt=April 21|1=April 21}}) – Tokugawa Iemochi entered Nijō Castle in Kyoto, the first time in 230 years since Shōkō had reigned. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}) – At Sunpu Castle, Tetsusaburō Yamaoka met with Saigō Takamori, appealing with a spirit of submission to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16|1=April 16}}) – The soldiers safeguarding the emperor—10,000 strong from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains—were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 10th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1697|en|1697|WD=Q7702|lt=1697|1=1697}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}) – Kamo Mafuchi, a Kokugaku scholar (<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mabuchi-kinenkan.jp/history/index.html|title=賀茂真淵について|publisher=賀茂真淵記念館|accessdate=2021-05-03|qq=}}</ref>) (died {{ill|1769|en|1769|WD=Q7679|lt=1769|1=1769}}). == Deaths ==

{{Not to be confused with|March 4|qq=March 4}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初四|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 4일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 4''' is the 4th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Shakkō|ja|赤口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakkō|1=Shakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635368}}.
== Events == * [[:en:天喜|天喜]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1053|en|1053|qid=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|qid=Q2456|lt=March 26|1=March 26}}) – The great Amitābha Hall (鳳凰堂) at Heidōin (平等院) in Uji was completed under the direction of Fujiwara Yorimichi. * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1771|en|1771|qid=Q7686|lt=1771|1=1771}} {{ill|April 18|en|April 18|qid=Q2521|lt=April 18|1=April 18}}) – Sugita Genpaku and Maeno Ryōtaku observed the dissection of condemned prisoners; the very next day they began translating the work “Tahel Anatomia.” * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|qid=Q7765|lt=1788|1=1788}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|qid=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – Tokugawa Iezawa appointed Matsudaira Sadatoki as his shōgun’s assistant (補佐), marking the beginning of the Kansei reforms. * [[嘉慶 (中国)|嘉慶]] 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1796|en|1796|qid=Q7818|lt=1796|1=1796}} {{ill|April 11|en|April 11|qid=Q2501|lt=April 11|1=April 11}}) – In the Qing dynasty, a rebellion was triggered by a group of Bai Lian (White Lotus) adherents—peasant groups forming a Maitreya-based religious association. * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1806|en|1806|qid=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} {{ill|April 22|en|April 22|qid=Q2526|lt=April 22|1=April 22}}) – A great fire, known as the “Great Cultural Fire,” broke out in Edo. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1837|en|1837|qid=Q7608|lt=1837|1=1837}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|qid=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – Due to the Great Tenpō Famine, the Tokugawa shogunate established rice relief granaries throughout Edo. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|qid=Q2525|lt=April 21|1=April 21}}) – Tokugawa Iemochi entered Nijō Castle in Kyoto, the first time in 230 years since Shōkō had reigned. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}) – At Sunpu Castle, Tetsusaburō Yamaoka met with Saigō Takamori, appealing with a spirit of submission to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|qid=Q2519|lt=April 16|1=April 16}}) – The soldiers safeguarding the emperor—10,000 strong from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains—were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 10th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1697|en|1697|qid=Q7702|lt=1697|1=1697}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|qid=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}) – Kamo Mafuchi, a Kokugaku scholar (<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mabuchi-kinenkan.jp/history/index.html|title=賀茂真淵について|publisher=賀茂真淵記念館|accessdate=2021-05-03|qq=}}</ref>) (died {{ill|1769|en|1769|qid=Q7679|lt=1769|1=1769}}). == Deaths ==
